Through original programming, documentaries, and expert-led discussions, MHTN aims to increase awareness, provide valuable information, and encourage open conversations about mental well-being.

programme.title: Quo Vadis TV
programme.desc: Quo Vadis TV is committed to sharing the Biblical message in a way that makes Christian living practical, realistic, and attainable. Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

programme.title: King of Kings
programme.desc: In this reenactment of the life of Jesus Christ, when word spreads throughout Judea that the son of God is to be born in Bethlehem, King Herod demands that all infants be killed. Mary (Siobhan McKenna) steals away with her young son, Jesus, who grows up preaching, performing miracles and acquiring devotees. One of Jesus' (Jeffrey Hunter) followers, Judas (Rip Torn), betrays him, and he is sentenced to crucifixion. But Jesus has always known of his fate and has prepared himself for death.

programme.title: Paid Programming: Carshield -- Cars Have Changed
The Kid From Broken Gun
programme.desc: After years on the East Coast, former boxer Jack Mahoney (Jack Mahoney) returns to New Mexico and finds himself framed for murder. Though the only evidence is circumstantial, in the face of vicious prosecutor Kiefer (Myron Healey), Mahoney's girlfriend -- defense lawyer Gail Kingston (Angela Stevens) -- fails to convince the jury of his innocence. Determined to clear Mahoney's name, postal worker Steve Reynolds (Charles Starrett) transforms himself into the masked rider known as the Durango Kid.

programme.title: Santa Fe
programme.desc: Three brothers become outlaws after the Civil War, while a fourth sibling goes to work for the railroad.

programme.title: The Charge at Feather River
programme.desc: A frontier scout (Guy Madison) leads prisoners on a death mission to save a railroad and rescue two women.

programme.title: Escape From Fort Bravo
programme.desc: Captain Roper (William Holden) is the warden of a Union-run prison camp. A mysterious woman named Carla Forester (Eleanor Parker) arrives at the camp in order to free the Confederate prisoners, and things get complicated when Carla and the captain begin to fall for each other. In an effort to recapture the escaped prisoners, Roper has a run-in with a group of Mescalero Indians. In order to survive, he has to team up with his own prisoners to fight the Indians and wipe them out.

programme.title: Hellfighters
programme.desc: An oil-rig firefighter's (John Wayne) estranged daughter (Katharine Ross) falls in love with his right-hand man (Jim Hutton).

programme.title: King of Kings
programme.desc: Orson Welles narrates the story of Jesus (Jeffrey Hunter) including the Sermon on the Mount, the Last Supper, the Passion and the Resurrection.

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Kent Taggart's family members, with their cattle stampeded, are killed by those who started it. In a fair gunfight, he kills the man's son responsible for it, and when he runs, a warrant is issued and a price is put on his head.

programme.title: Bandits of El Dorado
programme.desc: The Durango Kid fakes a murder in the hopes of learning how fugitives escape across the Mexican border.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: A gambler tries to palm his troubles off on his brother-in-law, an ex-con trying to go straight.

programme.desc: Tired of bloating or gut issues that won't go away?; discovering the root cause-and how Emma can help eliminate methane-producing archaea, reset digestion and restore balance naturally.

programme.title: Ministry - Your Words Have Power with Joyce Meyer
programme.desc: Your words hold incredible power; today, Joyce reveals how the things you say can impact your life in profound ways.

programme.title: Word of God Ministries
programme.desc: Pastor James McMenis and his wife Chrissy are dedicated to winning the lost to Jesus and making disciples of the saved. Their goal is to bring clarity to the Word of God, making it life-applicable to people everywhere.

programme.title: Perry Stone

programme.desc: 2026 will be one of the most historic years ever in U.S. coinage; join your host Mike Mezack to learn about the incredible U.S. Quarter program and get a chance at an exclusive P&D Roll!

programme.title: Escape From Fort Bravo
programme.desc: A Southern belle (Eleanor Parker) frees a Rebel officer (John Forsythe) and his men from a Union captain's (William Holden) Arizona fort.

programme.title: Decision at Sundown
programme.desc: A gunman (Randolph Scott) rides into Sundown for a showdown with the big shot (John Carroll) he blames for stealing his wife.

programme.title: Samson and Delilah
programme.desc: The mighty Samson (Victor Mature) finds his strength and power tested by the manipulative charms of lovely but devious Delilah (Hedy Lamarr).

programme.title: The Robe
programme.desc: The red robe worn by Christ links a Roman tribune (Richard Burton), his Christian lover (Jean Simmons) and a Greek slave (Victor Mature).

programme.title: The Lonesome Trail
programme.desc: A group of conniving land-grabbers is deterred from further evil by a Western Robin Hood armed with a bow and arrow.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Outlaws recruit Doc Holliday in their efforts to frame crooked sheriff Johnny Behan.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: It's free enterprise to the extreme as rival saloonkeepers compete for customers.

programme.title: Samson and Delilah
programme.desc: Samson (Victor Mature), the hero of the Israelites, wins the hand of a Philistine woman, Semadar (Angela Lansbury), in a contest of strength. When she is killed during a conflict on her wedding night, however, Samson becomes a hunted man whom the Philistines, including Semadar's sister, Delilah (Hedy Lamarr), want to punish. This becomes complicated, however, when Samson suddenly acquires God-given superpowers, and Delilah's true feelings for Samson surface.

programme.title: The Robe
programme.desc: In this biblical epic, a drunk and disillusioned Roman, Marcellus Gallio (Richard Burton), wins Jesus' robe in a dice game after the crucifixion. Marcellus has never been a man of faith like his slave, Demetrius (Victor Mature), but when Demetrius escapes with the robe, Marcellus experiences disturbing visions and feels guilty of his actions. Convinced that destroying the robe will cure him, Marcellus sets out to find Demetrius -- and discovers his Christian faith along the way.

programme.title: The Last Days of Frank and Jesse James
programme.desc: The outlaw brothers (Kris Kristofferson, Johnny Cash) go from the Civil War to robbing trains to farming under assumed names.

programme.title: Decision at Sundown
programme.desc: Hell-bent on revenge, flinty gunslinger Bart Allison (Randolph Scott) rides into a sleepy Western town with one goal in mind: to kill local roughneck Tate Kimbrough (John Carroll), who kidnapped his wife years ago. Both men have blood on their hands over the woman's eventual suicide. Allison and Kimbrough, wracked with guilt but boiling over with bloodlust, are set to face off for one final confrontation. Tensions mount as sunset approaches, and the townspeople must choose sides.

programme.title: Smoke Signal
programme.desc: An Army deserter (Dana Andrews) and his girlfriend (Piper Laurie) shoot Grand Canyon rapids with his cavalry captors, chased by Indians.

programme.title: The Victim
programme.desc: A rich woman (Elizabeth Montgomery) goes to her sister's house during a storm and is trapped inside with a killer.

programme.title: Designing Woman
programme.desc: Sports reporter Mike (Gregory Peck) and clothes designer Marilla (Lauren Bacall) meet and fall in love while on vacation. They marry on a whim, only to come to the shocking realization that they have very little in common. Mike is a blue-collar sports fanatic. Marilla is a high-society fashion diva. But when Mike writes a tell-all piece about a boxing promoter (Edward Platt) with mob ties, they have to overcome their differences to save both their relationship and Mike's life.

programme.title: Angel in My Pocket
programme.desc: After minister Sam Whitehead (Andy Griffith) moves to his new post in a small Kansas town, he finds that the place is in total gridlock because of a feud between the families of Mayor Will Sinclair (Henry Jones) and his rival Axel Gresham (Edgar Buchanan). Meanwhile, Sam's reckless brother-in-law, Bubba (Jerry Van Dyke), turns the church boiler into an alcohol still. Sam is ready to quit when his friend Art (Gary Collins) decides to enter politics in order to change the town for the better.

programme.title: Weekend Recharge
programme.desc: Though it has "weekend" in the title, this show discusses the week ahead in addition to offering suggestions for what to do on Saturday and Sunday. Weather Channel meteorologists go beyond the elements outside and into the realm of lifestyle tips. Each host has a recurring segment on "Weekend Recharge," which includes helping parents get a fresh start to each day and explaining how the weather is expected to impact the weekend's scheduled sports action.

programme.desc: On a winter night in South Carolina's Lowcountry, a simple boat trip turns into an unforgettable nightmare that triggers the demise of the Murdaughs, a prominent South Carolina family.

programme.title: Storm of Suspicion
programme.desc: On a cold November night, a man calls the police because his wife went out for a walk and never returned; when police finally find her body, it tells a far different story than a simple walk in the snow.

programme.title: Storm of Suspicion
programme.desc: On July 26, 2002, Ty Lathon was killed by a shotgun blast near the Shenandoah River. Tire tracks in the muddy scene led police to suspect Lewis Felts. Surveillance caught Felts cleaning his Jeep, and forensic geologists matched unique mud on his car to the crime scene. Phone records placed him nearby. Faced with the evidence, Felts pled guilty to murder and received 25 years.

programme.title: Think Big
programme.desc: Children are the future -- and so are their inventions, which is why "Think Big" could be an important show. It features young inventors competing to see who can come up with the most-innovative invention. Each episode features two teams in the Think Tank -- a studio filled with art supplies and construction materials -- who brainstorm and choose materials before sketching, designing and building a prototype to be presented to a judge, who determines a winner. The champions earn bragging rights and the coveted Genius Cup.

programme.title: Biz Kids
programme.desc: Teaching financial education and entrepreneurship to a preteen audience; sketch comedy and young actors help explain basic economic concepts.

programme.title: Sports Stars of Tomorrow
programme.desc: Profiles of the top rookie, college and high-school talent in sports; current athletes share their experiences, advice and personal impressions of the road to stardom.

programme.title: America's Heartland
programme.desc: Much of the food Americans eat is produced by farmers and ranchers in the country's heartland, but many people don't know how it's produced. "America's Heartland" provides information about the people and processes responsible for the availability of food and fuel across the country and around the world. The show's reporters and producers tell stories in topics that include farm families, consumer issues, animal welfare and crop sustainability. Some episodes also take the show abroad to countries such as Egypt and Taiwan to show the impact American agriculture has on the global economy.

programme.title: Ducks Unlimited
programme.desc: Alaska the last frontier, need we say more? On this episode Co-Host Colin Mulligan and the DUTV crew make the long journey to the rugged state of Alaska for an incredible bucket list waterfowl adventure....

programme.title: Heartland Waterfowl
programme.desc: In the cold of Torrington, Wyo., the crew teams up with High Plains Wingshooters for late-flying geese and all-day pit blind grinds; it's a battle of patience, but the rewards come heavy culminating in fresh pushes of new birds.

programme.title: Angling Edge
programme.desc: Al Lindner and Johnny Candle hit North Dakota's Devils Lake, catching piles of shallow-water walleyes on hardbaits during early summer.

programme.title: Big Water Angler
programme.desc: Taking viewers on an adventurous journey across the Great Lakes region and beyond, exploring the best fishing destinations, techniques and insider tips from seasoned captains and guides.

programme.title: Bob Redfern's Outdoor Magazine
programme.desc: Outdoor sporting and recreational events for everyone.

programme.title: Bill Dance Saltwater
programme.desc: This week, Bill is battling big snook near Port Canaveral, Florida!

programme.title: Livin' the Dream With Captain Jimmy Nelson
programme.desc: Livin' the Dream follows Captain Jimmy Nelson and his wife Luiza as they travel and fish in exotic locales.

programme.title: Two Conchs Adventures
programme.desc: The Two Conchs crew, Sam Foley and his son fish for offshore mahi, grouper and snapper.

programme.title: The Hunt for Monster Bass
programme.desc: Learn lures and tactics from champion angler Ott DeFoe as he fishes his way to a monster largemouth.

programme.title: JT Kenney's Florida Bass
programme.desc: Join pro angler JT Kenney as he embarks on an epic journey across Florida, navigating its diverse waters in pursuit of monstrous bass, showcasing the thrill of the chase and the beauty of the Sunshine State's landscapes.

programme.title: Steve's Outdoor Adventures: Fishing with Wesley
programme.desc: Wesley White travels to Brazil to fish for Arapaima and before flying into the Rio Negro River.

programme.title: A Fishing Story With Ronnie Green
programme.desc: Julian Brown is an environmental innovator and entrepreneur who developed Plastoline, a technology that transforms plastic waste into usable fuel. He walks us through his journey as a pioneer that's changing the world.

programme.title: Jimmy Houston Outdoors
programme.desc: Fishing in Tampa Bay with Capt Ryan Reynolds.

programme.title: The Fishmonger
programme.desc: Tommy's back home at Tunaville Market slinging fish and a special delivery.

programme.title: Predator Pursuit
programme.desc: Part One - In Florida, it's all-out action with gator hunts by day and hog stalks by night using thermal gear. A few buddies join the adventure as guide Justin Guerry of Gator Glades Hunts leads the crew through nonstop pursuit and southern adrenaline.

programme.title: Predator Pursuit
programme.desc: Predator Pursuit TV, hosted by Jeff Thomason, is a rare blend of fast action predator hunting and educational material all rolled into one. Jeff will be taking viewers all over the world chasing a variety of dangerous predators. Prepare to be entertained, and informed, on different tactics to help you become a more successful hunter and put more fur in the back of your truck!

programme.title: Predator Pursuit
programme.desc: Part Three - In Florida, it's all-out action with gator hunts by day and hog stalks by night using thermal gear. A few buddies join the adventure as guide Justin Guerry of Gator Glades Hunts leads the crew through nonstop pursuit and southern adrenaline.

programme.title: Predator Pursuit
programme.desc: Jeff and his son Case team up with good friend Kip in the heart of Texas for nonstop gray fox action, running day and night to stay on the pursuit.

programme.title: Predator Pursuit
programme.desc: This week, Jeff hunts at Three Curl Outfitters, battling wind, fog, and tough conditions while grinding out stand after stand in pursuit of coyotes and bobcats.

programme.title: Predator Pursuit

programme.title: Stompin' groundz
programme.desc: Black Bear Island Lodge lives up to its name for a pair of seasoned bow hunters.

programme.title: Jim Shockey's Hunting Adventures
programme.desc: Jim loves traveling to unusual places to hunt unusual animals so naturally a trip to Ethiopia is right up his alley.

programme.title: This Is Africa

programme.desc: Darrick takes his turn in the Colorado high country as patience pays off on a memorable elk adventure.

programme.title: Our Way of Life

programme.title: Dead Meat

programme.title: Jim Shockey's Hunting Adventures
programme.desc: Jim loves traveling to unusual places to hunt unusual animals so naturally a trip to Ethiopia is right up his alley.

programme.title: Fight to Survive
programme.desc: Faith, determination and human spirit abound in "Fight to Survive," which recounts stories of people's deadly ordeals in the outdoors. Accompanying survivors back to where harrowing events occurred is rock-climber Craig DeMartino, who is featured in the premiere episode. His right leg was amputated below the knee after a climbing accident, but he continued in his sport. In 2006 he became the first amputee to free-climb the 3,200-foot face of Yosemite's El Capitan in one day. Other stories tell of sailor Steven Callahan clinging to a raft in the Atlantic, fisherman Dean Ririe trapped by a boulder in icy water, and hiker Ted Porter falling into a glacial crevasse.

programme.title: Justice With Judge Mablean
programme.desc: Judge Mablean Ephriam, who presided over "Divorce Court" from 1999-2006 as the first star of the revived version of the show, returns to the courtroom genre with his half-hour series that deals with life and the law. The former Los Angeles-based prosecutor takes on the typical cases that are found on TV court shows. The arbitrator says that her show "will be life because everything we do, it involves the law."

programme.title: The Verdict With Judge Hatchett
programme.desc: Judge Glenda Hatchett presided over her self-titled courtroom show from 2000-2008. Now, she returns to the medium with "The Verdict." In the half-hour series, the Atlanta native and former juvenile-court judge rules over small-claims cases brought in front of her. Hatchett's courtroom isn't just about finding someone guilty, though. She also uses her experience dealing with young people and her tough-love approach to help send a message to out-of-control juvenile offenders to try to set them on the right track in their lives.

programme.title: Supreme Justice With Judge Karen
programme.desc: Karen Mills was a county court judge and criminal defense attorney in Florida before turning to TV to preside over her own court shows. In her third effort, "Supreme Justice," Mills tackles small-claims court arbitrations in her compassionate way, to which viewers of her previous shows have grown accustomed. As usual with court shows, cases involve friendships gone bad, money disputes and property damage.
